encounter.reason: Third pregnancy confirmation and early monitoring
encounter.location: Rocky Mountain Women's Health, Denver, CO

labs.name: crown-rump length by ultrasound
labs.interpretation: consistent with 6 weeks 1 day gestational age

screening.name: PHQ-2 depression screening total score
screening.clinical_context: patient endorsed persistent worry and difficulty sleeping related to pregnancy anxiety

medications.name: micronized progesterone 200 mg vaginal capsule
medications.sig: Insert 200 mg vaginally every night at bedtime
medications.reason: precautionary supplementation for recurrent early pregnancy loss

medications.name: prenatal vitamin with folate, one tablet by mouth daily

document_references.description: Documents third pregnancy confirmation at ~5 weeks gestation in G3P0 patient with history of two prior first-trimester losses. Records positive 6-week transvaginal ultrasound showing fetal cardiac activity, initiation of vaginal micronized progesterone 200 mg nightly as precautionary supplementation, positive PHQ-2 screen for anxiety, and plan for close follow-up with 8-week ultrasound.

diagnostic_reports.type: transvaginal pelvic ultrasound, obstetric, first trimester
diagnostic_reports.description: Single intrauterine gestational sac with fetal pole identified. Fetal cardiac activity present, FHR 112 bpm. Crown-rump length 4.2 mm, consistent with 6 weeks 1 day gestational age. No adnexal masses or free fluid. Reassuring findings in setting of recurrent early pregnancy loss.
